children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary, working through the NTIA, and the Commission shall identify a total of at least 255 megahertz of Federal and non-Federal spectrum for mobile and fixed wireless broadband use.December 31, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: 100 megahertz below the frequency of 8000 megahertz shall be identified for use on an unlicensed basis;

children.label: B
children.text: 100 megahertz below the frequency of 6000 megahertz shall be identified for use on an exclusive, licensed basis for commercial mobile use, pursuant to the Commission’s authority to implement such licensing in a flexible manner, and subject to potential continued use of such spectrum by incumbent Federal entities in designated geographic areas indefinitely or for such length of time stipulated in transition plans approved by the Technical Panel under for those incumbent entities to be relocated to alternate spectrum; andsection 923(h) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: 55 megahertz below the frequency of 8000 megahertz shall be identified for use on either a licensed or unlicensed basis, or a combination of licensed and unlicensed.

children.label: A
children.text: The frequencies between 1695 and 1710 megahertz.

children.label: B
children.text: The frequencies between 1755 and 1780 megahertz.

children.label: C
children.text: The frequencies between 2155 and 2180 megahertz.

children.label: D
children.text: The frequencies between 3550 and 3700 megahertz.

children.label: E
children.text: Spectrum that the Commission determines had more than de minimis mobile or fixed wireless broadband operations within the band on the day before .March 23, 2018

children.heading: Treatment of certain other spectrum
children.label: 4 Treatment of certain other spectrum
children.text: Spectrum identified pursuant to this section may include eligible spectrum, if any, identified after , pursuant to title X of the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015 ().March 23, 2018Public Law 114–74

children.heading: Spectrum made available on and afterFebruary 11, 2016
children.label: 5 Spectrum made available on and afterFebruary 11, 2016
children.text: Any spectrum that has been made available for licensed or unlicensed use on and after , and that otherwise satisfies the requirements of this section may be counted towards the requirements of this subsection.February 11, 2016

children.heading: Relocation prioritized over sharing
children.label: 6 Relocation prioritized over sharing
children.text: This section shall be carried out in accordance with .section 923(j) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the need to preserve critical existing and planned Federal Government capabilities;

children.label: B
children.text: the impact on existing State, local, and tribal government capabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: the international implications;

children.label: D
children.text: the need for appropriate enforcement mechanisms and authorities; and

children.label: E
children.text: the importance of the deployment of wireless broadband services in rural areas of the United States.
to impair or otherwise affect the functions of the Director of OMB relating to budgetary, administrative, or legislative proposals;
to require the disclosure of classified information, law enforcement sensitive information, or other information that must be protected in the interest of national security; or

to affect any requirement under section 156 of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act ( note), as added by section 1062(a) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2000, or any other relevant statutory requirement applicable to the reallocation of Federal spectrum.47 U.S.C. 921
